items.q: What is hexadecimal A in denary?
items.why: Students forget hex uses A-F to represent 10-15 because we need 16 distinct symbols. A=10, B=11, C=12, D=13, E=14, F=15.

items.q: Why must a recursive function have a base case?
items.correct: Without it, the function calls itself indefinitely, causing a stack overflow.
items.random_wrong: To make the function run faster.
items.why: Students treat the base case as optional ceremony. It's the TERMINATION CONDITION that stops recursion - each call adds a stack frame, and without termination the stack will overflow.

items.q: What is the worst-case complexity of bubble sort?
items.misconception: O(n) because it just passes through the list.
items.why: Students count one pass and forget that bubble sort does up to n passes, each comparing up to n elements. The nested loop structure gives n times n = n squared.

items.q: Simula 67, introducing classes and objects, was released in:
items.why: It's 1967. “1958” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: UML was standardised by OMG in:
items.why: Correct answer: 1997. “1991” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Year Simula 67 was released, introducing OOP concepts?
items.why: Exact answer: 1967 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
